source.description: Where the fact came from: user / tool / chat / library:… . Optional; drives the source-priority precedence policy.

confidence.description: Salience/confidence in 0..1 on write. Optional; defaults to 0.5.

reason.description: Why this assertion replaces the previous one — the audit reason recorded on the superseded fact. Optional; a default is used.

ok.description: True when the fact was recorded (whether or not it superseded one); false on auth/scope/validation error.

subject.description: Canonical subject key the fact is about (lowercased/trimmed).

source.description: Where the fact came from (chat / library:… / tool / user).

status.description: "active" for the current truth, "superseded" once replaced.

resolution.description: Why this fact was superseded; null while active.

precedence.description: Which policy decided supersession (recency/source-priority/intent-aware); null while active.

fact.description: The new (or refreshed) active fact. Present when ok is true.

subject.description: Canonical subject key the fact is about (lowercased/trimmed).

source.description: Where the fact came from (chat / library:… / tool / user).

status.description: "active" for the current truth, "superseded" once replaced.

resolution.description: Why this fact was superseded; null while active.

precedence.description: Which policy decided supersession (recency/source-priority/intent-aware); null while active.

superseded.description: The previously-active fact that this one replaced, with its resolution/precedence. Absent on an idempotent no-op or first assertion.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: factHistoryTool
tools.description: Read the audit trail for one subject — current active value plus the full superseded chain (newest to oldest), each with why it changed and which precedence policy decided it. Read-only; requires read scope.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated; the server resolves auth from the session.

sessionId.description: Session identifier used to resolve the active vault and per-session state. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

vault.description: Vault to read from. Optional; defaults to the session active vault, then the first vault the caller is entitled to.

subject.description: The subject whose history to read (e.g. "db preference"). Canonicalized to match how it was recorded.

ok.description: True when the history was read; false on an auth/scope error.

value.description: The asserted value/conclusion at this point in the chain.

source.description: Where it came from (chat / library:… / tool / user).

status.description: "active" for the current truth, "superseded" for a replaced assertion.

resolution.description: Why this assertion was superseded (the audit reason); null while active.

precedence.description: Which policy decided it (recency/source-priority/intent-aware); null while active.

updatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last change to this fact.

current.description: The current active fact for the subject, or null if none exists. Present when ok is true.

value.description: The asserted value/conclusion at this point in the chain.

source.description: Where it came from (chat / library:… / tool / user).

status.description: "active" for the current truth, "superseded" for a replaced assertion.

resolution.description: Why this assertion was superseded (the audit reason); null while active.

precedence.description: Which policy decided it (recency/source-priority/intent-aware); null while active.

updatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last change to this fact.

history.description: The superseded chain newest → oldest — the audit trail. Empty when nothing has been superseded. Present when ok is true.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: storageUsageTool
tools.description: Report total storage used by the caller across every visible vault against their plan quota, with a per-vault breakdown. Bytes are note content plus search-embedding vectors; scoped to the caller so totals never leak other tenants. Read-only.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated; the server resolves auth from the session.

sessionId.description: Session identifier used to resolve the active vault and per-session state. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

ok.description: True when usage was computed; false on an auth/scope error.

totalBytes.description: Total stored bytes across all the caller vaults. Present when ok is true.

totalGb.description: Total stored size in decimal GB (bytes / 1e9), rounded to 2 decimals. Present when ok is true.

quotaBytes.description: Storage quota in bytes for the caller plan. Null when the plan is unlimited. Present when ok is true.

quotaGb.description: Storage quota in decimal GB, rounded to 2 decimals. Null when the plan is unlimited. Present when ok is true.

unlimited.description: True when the caller plan is unlimited (no storage ceiling). Render "Unlimited" instead of a quota figure.

vault.description: Vault name.

bytes.description: Stored bytes in this vault (octet_length sum).

gb.description: Stored size of this vault in decimal GB, rounded to 2 decimals.

notes.description: Number of notes stored in this vault.

perVault.description: Per-vault storage breakdown for the caller vaults. Present when ok is true.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: costUsageTool
tools.description: Report the caller's metered AI/infra cost for the current billing month (LLM + embeddings + storage + compute), storage vs. plan quota, and free-tier $1 cap status. Operators additionally receive per-plan blended cost for the margin guard. Read-only.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

period.description: Billing month as YYYY-MM. Optional; defaults to the current month.

ok.description: True when usage was computed; false on auth/scope error.

identity.description: The caller identity the costs are scoped to.

costUsd.description: Total metered cost this period in USD.

freeCapUsd.description: The free-tier monthly processing cap in USD.

freeCapReached.description: True when a free caller is at/over the cap (embeds/optimization paused).

proBudgetUsd.description: The Pro soft fair-use budget (price / 3) in USD.

totalBytes.description: Total stored bytes for the caller.

totalGb.description: Total stored GB for the caller.

quotaGb.description: Storage quota in GB for the caller plan. Null when the plan is unlimited.

unlimited.description: True when the caller is on the unlimited plan (no storage ceiling, exempt from the free cost cap).

byTier.description: Operator-only: blended cost-per-user per plan for the current period.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: createScheduledActionTool
tools.description: Create a scheduled action in agent mode (default) or connection_sync mode. Agent mode asks an agent to follow the description and write a result into the target vault. connection_sync deterministically runs the approved read-only tools on bound service connections and ingests their data; it requires at least one connection to be bound before execution. Cadence 'once' runs a single time then completes permanently. Requires an active scheduling subscription and write access to the target vault.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

description.description: Free-text description of what this action should do each time it runs.

vault.description: The vault this action writes its results into. You must already have write access to it.

cadence.description: How often this action runs. "once" fires a single time and then completes.

executionMode.description: How to execute each run. "agent" (default) lets an agent follow the description. "connection_sync" deterministically ingests data from the schedule's bound connections using only their approved read-only tools; bind at least one connection before it runs.

timeOfDay.description: 24-hour HH:MM clock time to run at, in the given timezone. Optional — omit to run at any time during the period (matches prior default behavior).

timezone.description: IANA timezone name, e.g. "America/Denver". Only meaningful together with timeOfDay or deployDate. Omit to use the account's default timezone (set via set-schedule-defaults), falling back to UTC.

deployDate.description: Calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD, in the given timezone) this action should first become eligible to run — its deployment/start date. For recurring cadences, the first occurrence lands on or after this date; every later occurrence still follows the normal cadence. For cadence "once", this (combined with timeOfDay if given) is exactly what day it fires. Omit to start immediately.

ok.description: True when the scheduled action was created.

executionMode.description: The stored execution mode. Defaults to agent when omitted from the request.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

code.description: Machine-readable denial code: not_enabled when no scheduling subscription is active.

tools.name: listScheduledActionsTool
tools.description: List every scheduled action you own — active, paused, and completed one-time actions — with execution mode, cadence, next run time, and last run status. connection_sync means deterministic read-only ingestion from bound service connections.

tools.name: pauseScheduledActionTool
tools.description: Pause a scheduled action so it stops running on its cadence, without deleting it.

id.description: The scheduled action id, from create-scheduled-action or list-scheduled-actions.

tools.name: resumeScheduledActionTool
tools.description: Resume a paused scheduled action. Its next run is computed fresh from now, not backfilled for time spent paused.

tools.name: deleteScheduledActionTool
tools.description: Permanently delete a scheduled action. It will not run again.

tools.name: setScheduleEntitlementTool
tools.description: Set whether an identity has an active Scheduled Actions subscription, its monthly execution quota, and (optionally) an mcp-scraper API key for scheduled actions to call mcp-scraper on their behalf. Called by mcp-scraper's Stripe webhook on subscribe/renew/cancel. Requires admin scope.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

granteeIdentity.description: Identity whose scheduling entitlement is being set (e.g. an email).

enabled.description: True to enable scheduled actions for this identity, false on cancel/expire.

quotaPerPeriod.description: Monthly execution quota. Optional; defaults to 1000, or leaves the existing value unchanged if already set.

mcpScraperApiKey.description: The identity's mcp-scraper API key, stored encrypted, used to reach mcp-scraper tools during scheduled-action execution.

ok.description: True when the entitlement was set; false on auth/scope error.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: getScheduleStatusTool
tools.description: Get your own Scheduled Actions subscription status: whether it is active, your monthly quota, how much you have used this period, and your default timezone for new schedules.

defaultTimezone.description: IANA timezone applied to new schedules that name a time without a zone; null means UTC.

tools.name: setScheduleDefaultsTool
tools.description: Set your default timezone for scheduled actions. Any schedule you later create with a time of day but no explicit timezone uses this default instead of UTC. Pass null to clear it back to UTC.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

defaultTimezone.description: IANA timezone name, e.g. "America/Denver". null clears the default (new schedules fall back to UTC).
The default timezone now in effect; null means UTC.

tools.name: proposeScheduledActionTool
tools.description: Turn freeform text describing what you want automated into a structured proposal (description, vault, cadence, time of day, timezone) for review and confirmation via create-scheduled-action — this is the propose step only; nothing is created here.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

request.description: Freeform text describing what you want scheduled.

description.description: Proposed description — edit freely before confirming.

vault.description: Proposed target vault.

cadence.description: Proposed cadence: once, daily, weekly, or monthly.

timeOfDay.description: Proposed 24-hour HH:MM run time, when the request named one.

timezone.description: Proposed IANA timezone — from the request, or the account default when the request named a time without a zone.

rationale.description: Why these choices were made — read this before confirming.

tools.name: getScheduleLinkTool
tools.description: Get your durable, bookmarkable link to the hosted Scheduled Actions page — a login-free UI to create, view, edit, pause, resume, and delete scheduled actions. The embedded secret is shown only once, on first call; it cannot be re-shown, only revoked and reissued via revoke-schedule-link.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

ok.description: True on success; false on auth error.

url.description: The schedule link. Present only the first time a link is minted for this identity.

alreadyExists.description: True when a link already exists and was NOT re-shown. Use revoke-schedule-link then call this again to get a fresh one.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: revokeScheduleLinkTool
tools.description: Revoke your existing Scheduled Actions link immediately — use if it was shared or leaked. Call get-schedule-link afterward to mint a fresh one.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

ok.description: True on success; false on auth error.

revoked.description: True if a link existed and was revoked; false if there was none to revoke.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false.

tools.name: createWebhookTool
tools.description: Create a standalone webhook URL that writes a note into one of your vaults whenever something POSTs to it — no MCP client or login required, so it works as a 'send to' target for forms, Zapier, or any webhook-capable tool. The secret is embedded in the URL, shown only once, and cannot be retrieved again — only revoked (revoke-webhook) and reissued.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

vault.description: Vault this webhook writes into. Optional; defaults to "Issues".

label.description: Optional human-readable label to help you remember what this webhook is for, e.g. "Website contact form".

url.description: The full webhook URL — POST here to write a note.

tools.name: listWebhooksTool
tools.description: List your webhooks — id, target vault, label, created time. The URL/secret itself is never shown again after creation.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

tools.name: revokeWebhookTool
tools.description: Permanently revoke a webhook by id — anything still POSTing to it starts getting rejected. Call create-webhook to make a replacement.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

tools.name: videoAnalyzeStartTool
tools.description: Start a deep async breakdown of a video: samples frames, transcribes audio, and runs parallel analyses (summary, pacing, WPM, topic outline, key points, hook analysis, visual style, replication recipe) into one saved report. Returns a runId immediately — poll video-analyze-status. Accepts a YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, or Vimeo URL directly (downloaded for you), or a direct video file URL (.mp4/.webm/.mov). Videos up to 1 hour.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.

sourceUrl.description: A YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, or Vimeo URL (downloaded automatically), or a direct video file URL (.mp4/.webm/.mov/.gif).

intervalS.description: Preferred seconds between sampled frames (1-30). Default 2. For long videos the interval is automatically widened so the whole video is covered within the frame budget. Lower = denser sampling where the video is short enough to allow it.

maxFrames.description: Hard cap on frames analyzed (≤480). Default 120. Frames are spread across the whole duration; lowest sampling interval is 1 second, so short videos cannot use more frames than their length in seconds.

vault.description: Vault to write the final breakdown note into. Default "Library". You must have write access.

tools.name: videoAnalyzeStatusTool
tools.description: Check the status of a video breakdown started with video-analyze-start. Returns phase and frame progress; when done, returns the saved report's vault path and markdown. Poll every few seconds until done or failed.

apiKey.description: Caller API key (Bearer secret). Optional when the MCP session is already authenticated.

sessionId.description: Session identifier. Optional; defaults to the current MCP session.
